description_long: This object is a pair of pliers featuring robust, curved handles coated in a deep red, likely rubberized material for enhanced grip and comfort. The handles are ergonomically shaped, widening at the base and narrowing toward the pivot, providing a secure hold during use. The jaws are made of dark metal and display a multi-functional design, with serrated gripping surfaces and notched sections for cutting or stripping wires. The pivot joint is prominent and fixed, ensuring stable movement of the jaws. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for various gripping, twisting, and cutting tasks commonly encountered in urban maintenance or repair settings. No visible branding or logos are present on the tool.
description: A sturdy pair of pliers with red ergonomic handles and dark metal, multi-purpose jaws featuring serrated and notched sections. The design emphasizes grip, comfort, and versatile functionality.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the red ergonomic handles and the open, serrated jaws with notched sections.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the handles and the alignment of the jaws, with the pivot joint visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the handle contours and the back of the jaws.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the handle profile and the side of the jaws.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, ergonomic handles with a red rubberized coating for improved grip and comfort.
attribute_jaw_design: Short, wide jaws with serrated gripping surfaces and notched sections for wire cutting or stripping; designed for general-purpose use rather than precision.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed pivot joint, providing stable, non-adjustable movement of the jaws.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth, rubberized surface on the handles with subtle ergonomic contouring; no visible ribbing or knurling.
